committee.name: Risk Manager
interests: Having a single, authoritative risk register that every department feeds into rather than a pile of spreadsheets
Seeing the current status of every open corrective action without chasing down the responsible manager
Being able to show auditors a timestamped, unedited history of how each risk was identified, assessed, and treated
Tracking which compliance obligations have been mapped to which internal controls, and flagging gaps
Getting early warning when a risk's inherent score changes because a new incident or corrective action was logged
Knowing which risks are overdue for re-assessment and who is accountable for the delay
Linking incidents to the root-cause risks so patterns surface before they become repeat findings
Reducing the manual prep work for quarterly risk committee reports
Having a defensible record of every risk decision — who approved a risk acceptance, when, and why
Seeing residual risk scores update automatically as controls close out, not just on the next scheduled review
decisionCriteria: The risk register must support custom likelihood/impact scales and multiple risk matrices, not lock me into one framework
Corrective actions must have hard due dates, assigned owners, escalation rules, and a clear audit trail of status changes
Compliance obligation mapping must show which specific controls satisfy each obligation and flag uncovered gaps
I need to export a full audit package — risk register, control evidence, incident log — without rebuilding it every time
The platform must distinguish between inherent risk, residual risk, and target risk at the individual risk level
Incident records need to attach directly to risks so I can see frequency of related events without manual cross-referencing
Role-based access so department managers can only update their own risks and corrective actions, not the whole register
Risk acceptance decisions must require formal sign-off with a named approver and an expiry date
The system must handle at least three frameworks simultaneously (e.g., ISO 31000, NIST, and a sector-specific one) without merging them into one undifferentiated list
Reporting output must be presentable to a non-technical risk committee without me reformatting it in PowerPoint first
goals: Eliminate the quarterly scramble to assemble audit-ready documentation by keeping records current in the platform all year
Cut the time I spend chasing overdue corrective action owners from hours per week to minutes
Give leadership a risk dashboard they can read in under five minutes before a board or committee meeting
Ensure every material risk has a documented treatment decision, an owner, and a next-review date — no orphaned risks
Reduce repeat audit findings by linking incidents back to root-cause risks and tracking whether corrective actions actually closed them
Move risk reassessment from an annual event to a rolling, triggered process when incidents or control failures occur
Make risk acceptance decisions traceable so I can answer exactly who signed off on any accepted risk and when
Provide department heads with a self-service view of their own risk and corrective-action portfolio so they stop asking me for status reports
Demonstrate to external auditors and insurers that the organization runs a systematic, documented risk program, not a reactive one
Shorten the time between an incident being logged and a corrective action being created and assigned
objections: If the import from our current spreadsheets doesn't carry over the full history, we lose five years of risk records and I can't defend that to auditors
We have three people on the risk team — if this takes more than a day to learn, it won't get used consistently
Our department managers already ignore email reminders; if the system can't escalate overdue actions automatically, nothing will change
I'm worried the risk scoring model is fixed and I'll spend months fighting the vendor to match our existing methodology
If every corrective action requires a separate login and workflow, the people responsible won't close them out — they'll wait for me to do it
We've tried GRC platforms before and they all became shelfware after the first audit season because maintenance was too heavy
The compliance obligation library needs to include our sector-specific regulations, not just the big generic frameworks
Reporting is always the weak point — if I have to rebuild the same risk heat map manually every quarter, this isn't saving me time
Our IT team is small; if this needs on-premise infrastructure or a complex integration to go live, it won't be approved
Budget approval will require me to show a concrete reduction in audit prep hours — I need to be able to measure that before renewal
openQuestions: When I log a new incident, does it automatically prompt me to link it to an existing risk, or do I have to go find the risk record separately?
How does the residual risk score recalculate — is it automatic when a corrective action closes, or do I have to trigger a manual re-assessment?
Can I configure our own likelihood and impact scales (we use a 5×5 matrix with custom descriptors), or is the scoring model locked?
If a corrective action is overdue, who gets notified — just the assignee, or does it escalate to their manager after a set number of days?
Can I map one compliance obligation to multiple controls, and see a coverage percentage for each regulation I'm tracking?
What does the audit export look like — is it a formatted report or a raw data dump, and can I choose which fields appear?
Can risk acceptance decisions require a named approver's signature and include an expiry date that triggers a re-review?
How do I handle a risk that spans two departments — can it have co-owners without creating duplicate records?
If I need to run three separate risk registers (operational, financial, compliance), can they live in one platform with separate reporting, or does that require separate instances?
What happens to the audit trail if someone edits a risk record after a finding has been documented — is the edit visible, timestamped, and attributable?
Can department managers update their corrective actions without having write access to the full risk register?
How far back does the incident history go, and can I filter incidents by risk category to see which areas generate the most corrective actions?
Is there a way to flag a risk as accepted rather than treated, and lock the record until the acceptance expires?
Can I schedule automated reminders for periodic risk reviews, and see a calendar view of what reviews are due in the next 90 days?
If our sector-specific regulation isn't in the default compliance library, how hard is it to add it and map our own controls to it?

committee.name: Operations Manager
interests: Knowing which operational risks and open corrective actions are sitting in my team's queue right now, without logging into a separate system
Having a clear view of which compliance tasks my department is responsible for and which are overdue
Getting concrete updates on incident investigations that touched my area, not just the initial report
Seeing corrective actions my team owns alongside their deadlines so I can plan capacity accordingly
Reducing the time I spend filling out risk and incident forms that no one ever seems to read
Understanding which of my processes have been flagged as high-risk so I can prioritize where to spend improvement effort
Having one place to close out corrective actions instead of responding to email threads and filling separate spreadsheets
Getting notified when a new risk or incident is assigned to my area rather than finding out during a quarterly review
Being able to show my own manager that my department's corrective actions are being closed on time
Seeing a simple status view — green/amber/red — across my department's risk and compliance obligations without needing to run a report
decisionCriteria: The corrective action workflow has to be something my team can complete in under two minutes — if it's cumbersome, they'll skip it
I need to see only my department's risks and actions by default, not wade through the entire enterprise register
Notifications need to be actionable — a link directly to the corrective action, not a generic alert that requires me to log in and search
If a deadline changes, I need to be able to request an extension with a reason attached, not just push the date without explanation
Mobile access or at least a clean browser interface that works without VPN, because my team isn't always at a desk
The system has to let me attach evidence — photos, completed checklists, sign-off documents — to a corrective action before I mark it closed
I don't want to re-enter information that was already captured in the incident report; corrective actions should pre-fill from the linked incident
Reporting should show my manager how quickly my team closes corrective actions versus the organizational average
I need to be able to delegate a corrective action to a team member without losing visibility of its status
If something is escalated above my level, I should still see it in my queue so I know it's being handled
goals: Have my department's compliance obligations and corrective actions visible in one place so I stop managing them across email, spreadsheets, and sticky notes
Close corrective actions faster by removing the back-and-forth of status update requests
Reduce the number of repeat incidents in my area by making it easy to trace open corrective actions back to the incidents that triggered them
Give my direct reports clear ownership of individual corrective actions so accountability is explicit
Avoid being caught off guard in a risk committee meeting by having a current view of my department's exposure before I walk in
Cut the time I spend preparing the quarterly operations compliance update from half a day to less than an hour
Make sure my team's corrective action closure rate is visible so credit is given for work that actually gets done
Have a simple way to flag when a corrective action is blocked — vendor delay, budget, staffing — so it doesn't just show as overdue without context
Reduce back-and-forth with the Risk Manager by being able to answer status questions myself from the platform
Ensure that when an auditor asks about a specific incident or corrective action, I can pull up the full history in under a minute
objections: My team is already stretched — if using this platform adds more data-entry steps than our current process, I'll get pushback immediately
We've had software rolled out before where the Risk team owns it and operations just gets alerts; that never works, we need real usability for frontline managers
If I have to log in separately from the tools my team already uses daily, adoption will drop off after the first month
The notifications need to be smarter than just 'action overdue' — if my team gets spammed, they'll tune them out
I'm concerned about who has visibility into my department's data — I don't want other departments seeing our incident details
If the corrective action form requires fields my team can't always fill out on day one, it'll block progress instead of enabling it
What happens when an action gets closed but the underlying problem wasn't actually fixed — does the system catch that, or does it just mark it green?
Training time matters — if this takes more than a half-day to learn for my team members, I can't get everyone through it
I need the mobile view to actually work, not just be a stripped-down read-only version that still requires desktop for anything real
Budget for this needs to be justified to my director — I need something concrete on time saved, not just a feature list
openQuestions: Can I see a filtered dashboard that shows only the risks, incidents, and corrective actions assigned to my department without customizing reports every time?
When a corrective action is created from an incident in my area, does the system pre-fill the details from the incident report or do I start from scratch?
If I want to delegate a corrective action to a team member, can I do that from the action record, and do I stay on the notification chain?
What does the evidence attachment process look like — can my team upload a photo from a phone or does it require a desktop upload?
If a corrective action is blocked — say, waiting on a vendor — how do I flag that so it doesn't just sit as overdue without explanation?
Can I request a deadline extension from inside the platform, with a reason, rather than going back to the Risk Manager over email?
What does my department's corrective-action closure rate look like in reporting, and can my manager pull that without asking me?
How do I know when a new risk or incident has been assigned to my department — is it an email, an in-app notification, both?
If my team closes a corrective action incorrectly or too early, can the Risk Manager reopen it and require additional evidence without creating a duplicate record?
Can I see the full incident-to-corrective-action thread in one view — what happened, what was assigned, what was done, when it closed?
Is there a way to see which of my team's corrective actions are due in the next two weeks without running a report?
If the same type of incident keeps happening in my department, does the system surface that pattern, or do I have to notice it myself?
Can I add an internal note to a corrective action that only my team sees, separate from the formal status update?
What happens if an incident spans two departments — are both managers notified, and can both departments update their part of the corrective action?
Is there a way to bulk-close corrective actions that are part of the same project once the underlying work is done, or do I have to close each one individually?

committee.name: Safety Manager
interests: Logging safety incidents and near-misses quickly, with enough structured detail to support a root-cause investigation
Tracking corrective actions from safety findings through to verified closure, with documented evidence
Mapping safety-related compliance obligations (OSHA, sector-specific regulations) to internal safety controls
Seeing which safety risks are overdue for re-assessment after an incident or regulatory update
Having an auditable record of every safety investigation finding, corrective action, and sign-off
Getting early warning when a corrective action tied to a safety risk is approaching its due date and hasn't been acted on
Linking repeat incidents back to the same root-cause risk so the pattern is visible in the risk register
Producing an inspection-ready compliance summary without rebuilding it from scratch before every audit
Tracking whether corrective actions actually eliminated the hazard or just papered over the finding
Having role-specific visibility so department supervisors can see and update their own safety corrective actions without touching other areas
decisionCriteria: Incident forms need fields specific to safety events — injury type, root cause category, immediate corrective action, contributing factors — not a generic form I have to bend to fit
Corrective actions from safety findings must have a verification step: closed doesn't mean done unless evidence is attached and reviewed
Compliance obligation tracking must cover OSHA recordkeeping requirements and our sector's inspection criteria, not just generic ISO or SOC frameworks
The audit trail on every safety incident must be immutable — I need to show a regulator exactly what was logged, when, by whom, and what changed
Notifications must escalate if a high-severity corrective action is approaching overdue — I can't be the only person chasing it
I need to run a regulatory inspection report that maps each finding to an open corrective action and shows current status, without reformatting the data
Safety risks need to carry a severity field separate from likelihood so a rare but catastrophic risk is visible at the top of the register
Department supervisors need to be able to log a near-miss from the floor without a Risk Manager account — contributor access with a simple form
The platform must support attaching photos, inspection checklists, and third-party audit reports as evidence on both incidents and corrective actions
If we're in an active regulatory investigation, I need to be able to generate a package of all relevant records — incident, findings, corrective actions, evidence — in a single export
goals: Eliminate the gap between when a safety incident happens and when it appears in the risk register as a linked corrective action
Give regulators and insurers a complete, unedited incident and corrective-action history on demand rather than assembling it manually
Reduce repeat incidents in high-risk areas by making it easy to see whether previous corrective actions in the same category actually worked
Ensure every safety corrective action has documented evidence of closure, not just a status change
Track safety compliance obligations continuously so I know our current gap status week to week, not only before an inspection
Make it easy for supervisors on the floor to log near-misses so the incident record is complete, not just the events that rose to my level
Demonstrate to leadership that the safety program is data-driven — incidents trend tracked, corrective actions closed on time, compliance obligations covered
Cut the time it takes to prepare a regulatory inspection package from two days to a few hours
Have a clear escalation path when a critical safety corrective action isn't being closed by the responsible manager
Create a defensible record that shows regulators and insurers we took every reported hazard seriously and followed through
objections: Safety incident forms in most GRC platforms are too generic — we'll spend months configuring fields before we get useful data
If supervisors on the floor can't log a near-miss without a full platform account, we'll miss 80% of the incidents we're supposed to be capturing
Corrective action verification has to be meaningful — if I can't require a named reviewer to sign off on closure evidence, 'closed' is just a checkbox
We've had regulators cite us for incomplete records before; if this platform allows edits to incident records without tracking the change, we're exposed
The compliance library needs OSHA and our sector-specific requirements out of the box — if I have to build the obligation list from scratch, that's months of setup
Notification fatigue is a real problem; if the system emails everyone about everything, safety notifications will get filtered the same as marketing
Our incident volume spikes during certain seasons — if the platform slows down or limits record volume, that's a non-starter
I'm worried about data ownership — if we switch platforms in three years, can we export a complete, regulator-legible history or are we locked in?
The cost needs to be justifiable relative to what we're spending now on manual tracking; I'll need to show a concrete reduction in audit prep or incident investigation time
If the platform requires IT to configure every notification rule and form field, changes will take months — I need to manage that myself
openQuestions: Does the incident form have safety-specific fields — injury type, root cause category, immediate action taken, contributing factors — or do I have to customize a generic form?
Can I require a verification step on corrective action closure so that the record doesn't close until a named reviewer approves the attached evidence?
If a supervisor on the floor needs to log a near-miss, what's the minimum access level required — do they need a full account or is there a simplified submission path?
When I look at a safety risk, can I see every incident that has been linked to it over the last two years, with dates and corrective action outcomes?
Does the compliance library include OSHA recordkeeping requirements and our sector-specific inspection criteria, or do I have to build that from scratch?
What does the regulatory inspection export look like — can I produce a single package with the incident, the findings, every corrective action, and the closure evidence?
If a high-severity corrective action is three days from its due date and hasn't been touched, who gets notified and can I configure that escalation path myself?
Can I mark a risk as safety-critical with a separate severity score so it stays at the top of the register regardless of its likelihood score?
What happens if an incident record needs to be amended after it's been submitted — is the original preserved, is the change tracked, and who can authorize the amendment?
Can I attach photos taken from a phone directly to a corrective action or incident record, or does evidence have to come from a desktop upload?
If we're in an active regulatory investigation, can I lock an incident record so it can't be edited while the investigation is open?
How do I track corrective action effectiveness — if the same type of incident recurs after an action was marked closed, does the system surface that connection?
Can department supervisors see and update their own safety corrective actions without having access to other departments' incident records?
Is there a calendar or timeline view showing which safety compliance obligations have review or reporting deadlines coming up in the next 60 days?
If we add a new sector-specific regulation that isn't in the default library, how do I add it and map our existing controls to it without vendor involvement?

committee.name: Administration Manager
interests: Understanding what ongoing administrative effort the platform requires to stay current and audit-ready
Knowing who has access to what, and how user provisioning and deprovisioning are handled as staff turn over
Making sure the platform's data-retention and record-keeping behavior lines up with our document policies
Getting clarity on what the implementation process looks like so I can schedule it without disrupting daily operations
Understanding what happens to our existing risk and compliance records during migration — and who owns that work
Having a clear picture of vendor support response times and what the escalation path looks like if the platform goes down before an audit
Knowing whether the subscription covers all the modules we need or whether core features like compliance mapping are add-on costs
Making sure the contract terms don't lock us in before we've proven the platform works for our risk team
Understanding the training requirements and whether the vendor provides materials or we're building our own
Being clear on data sovereignty — where records are stored, whether we can export a full archive, and what happens if we cancel
decisionCriteria: Total cost of ownership must be predictable — no surprise per-incident fees, per-user overages, or add-on module charges for features described as standard
Implementation must be scoped with a realistic timeline that doesn't require months of configuration before the risk team can start using it
User access management must support role-based permissions I can manage from an admin panel without submitting support tickets
Data export must produce a complete, structured archive we can retain independently — I need to know our records aren't held hostage by the vendor
Vendor support must include a service level that covers business hours at minimum, with a named escalation path for time-sensitive issues
Contract must not auto-renew without advance notice, and the initial term should be no longer than 12 months until we've validated fit
Onboarding documentation and user training materials must be included, not a separate paid engagement
The platform must handle multi-user concurrency without data conflicts — several managers updating corrective actions simultaneously can't create record integrity problems
Any integrations we add (HR, ticketing, document management) need to be standard connectors, not custom engineering
Vendor must be able to provide a data processing agreement and confirm GDPR/state privacy compliance before we sign
goals: Complete implementation without disrupting the risk team's existing workflows during the transition period
Establish a clear, low-overhead admin routine so the platform stays current without requiring a dedicated administrator
Ensure that when staff change roles or leave, their access is updated or revoked quickly without leaving open permissions in the system
Have a documented backup and export process so we're never dependent on the vendor to recover records we generated
Provide leadership with a clear implementation timeline and cost summary so sign-off can happen without repeated back-and-forth
Reduce the administrative overhead of maintaining compliance records by consolidating what's currently spread across email threads, shared drives, and spreadsheets
Make sure the platform's retention behavior matches our document management policy so we're not keeping records longer or shorter than required
Get a signed data processing agreement in place before any employee or incident data is entered
Establish a support contact and escalation path before go-live so the risk team isn't left without help during the first audit season
Confirm the renewal and cancellation terms so there's no administrative surprise at the end of the first contract year
objections: If pricing is per-user and our risk and compliance team expands, the cost could double without any change in the scope of work we originally scoped
We've signed software contracts where the implementation was described as simple and took six months — I need a realistic estimate, not a sales timeline
Data migration from our current spreadsheets needs to be scoped clearly; if it's billed separately, the total cost will be higher than what's been presented
If user provisioning requires a support ticket every time someone joins or leaves, the access list will drift and we'll have a control issue
I need a clear statement on where data is stored and whether we can pull a full archive on demand — not a general privacy policy that doesn't answer the question
The contract terms need to allow us to exit after year one if the risk team decides it's not working, without a multi-year penalty
Training materials have to be practical for operational managers, not a 40-page manual that no one reads before the first audit
If the platform has planned downtime during business hours, that needs to be in the SLA — not just 'we try to minimize disruption'
I'm cautious about signing before the Risk Manager has had hands-on time with the platform — I don't want to commit based on a demo alone
If add-on modules are needed for compliance mapping or reporting, I need to know that now, not after go-live
openQuestions: What does the per-user pricing model look like — are there different tiers for full users versus people who only close corrective actions?
Is data migration from spreadsheets included in the implementation, or is it scoped and billed separately?
What does user provisioning look like — can I add and remove users and adjust their permissions from an admin panel, or does that require a support request?
If we need to export our full record archive — every risk, incident, corrective action, and audit trail — what format does that come in and how long does it take to generate?
What's the realistic implementation timeline for a team our size, and what are the main milestones that could push it out?
Is there a data processing agreement available, and does it confirm GDPR compliance and state where data is stored?
What is the support SLA — business hours only, 24/7, or tiered by severity — and who do we contact if the platform is down the morning of an audit?
Does the subscription include all the modules we've seen in the demo — compliance mapping, incident management, corrective actions — or are any of those add-ons?
What is the auto-renewal policy, and how much advance notice do we get before the renewal date?
If we cancel, how do we retrieve our data, and is there a guaranteed window during which our records remain accessible after the subscription ends?
What training resources does the vendor provide, and are they included or a separate paid engagement?
If we add an integration to our HR system for user provisioning, is that a standard connector or a custom development project?
How are planned maintenance windows handled — are they scheduled outside business hours, and is there advance notice?
What is the minimum contract term, and is there a pilot or short-term option before committing to an annual subscription?
If we add users or modules mid-term, how is the additional cost calculated and when is it billed?

committee.name: Department Manager
interests: Seeing only the risks, compliance obligations, and corrective actions that belong to my department, not the whole organization
Understanding what the platform is actually asking me to do and how long it will take on a given week
Getting notified when a new corrective action is assigned to my team rather than discovering it in a meeting
Being able to close out corrective actions myself without having to go through the Risk Manager for every update
Knowing which deadlines are coming up across my team's corrective action list so I can plan workload
Having a simple way to explain to my team members what they need to do and why it matters
Seeing whether the actions my team has closed have actually stayed closed or triggered follow-up
Getting credit for work my team does — if we close actions on time, I want that visible in reporting
Not having to relearn a complicated system every time a new compliance cycle starts
Being able to attach proof of completion — a completed checklist, a photo, a policy document — when I mark something done
decisionCriteria: The interface for closing a corrective action must be simple enough that a manager without GRC background can do it correctly the first time
My department view must filter automatically to my team's items — I shouldn't have to configure that every time I log in
Notifications must tell me exactly what is due, when, and what I need to do — not just that something is pending
Attaching evidence to a corrective action must work from a standard browser without special software or desktop-only restrictions
If a deadline needs to change for a legitimate reason, I need a way to request that with an explanation, not just push the date
Reporting that shows my department's closure rate must be accessible to me, not only to the Risk Manager
The system should not require me to fill in fields that only make sense for a risk professional — the forms for my role need to be scoped to what I actually know
If I have a question about a risk or corrective action assigned to my team, I need to be able to see the context — what triggered it, what the expectation is — without calling the Risk Manager
The platform must work on a standard laptop browser; no special client installs that require IT involvement
The time I spend in the platform each week should be proportional to actual work — five minutes to close an action, not five minutes to navigate to it
goals: Spend less time managing compliance paperwork and more time on actual department work, without compliance tasks falling through the cracks
Have a clear, current list of what my team is accountable for so I can answer the Risk Manager's questions without a separate conversation
Close corrective actions efficiently and have the record show that my team responded on time
Reduce the stress of audit preparation by having records in order throughout the year, not just in the two weeks before an auditor arrives
Make it easy for my team members to understand their individual responsibilities without me having to translate GRC language into plain terms
Avoid being the reason a corrective action shows as overdue because I didn't know it existed or what I needed to do
Have confidence that when I mark something closed, the record is correct and won't be reopened because I missed an evidence requirement
See patterns in my department's corrective actions so I can address recurring issues proactively rather than reacting each time
Give my own manager a clean view of how our department handles compliance obligations so it doesn't reflect poorly on my team
Have a reliable way to hand off a corrective action to a team member with clear instructions, so I'm not the bottleneck for every closure
objections: If this platform adds more work to my week without a clear reduction elsewhere, I'm going to struggle to keep up with it beyond the first month
I've been sent spreadsheets, shared drives, and email trackers for compliance tasks before — I need this to be genuinely simpler, not just a fancier version of the same overhead
My team doesn't have GRC expertise; if the forms ask for risk assessment language they don't understand, they'll fill them in wrong or skip them
If the notifications are too frequent or too vague, my team will start ignoring them and I'll be back to chasing manually
I'm worried I'll mark something closed, get it right from my perspective, and then have it reopened weeks later because a verification step I didn't know about wasn't satisfied
Training needs to be short and practical — a 20-minute walkthrough, not a full-day course — or I won't get my whole team through it
I don't want to be responsible for data entry that belongs to the Risk Manager's function; my role should be updating status and attaching evidence, not creating risk records
If I can't see what triggered a corrective action — the original incident or risk — I won't be able to explain to my team why it matters
Access permissions need to be right from day one — I don't want team members seeing sensitive incident records from other departments
If this becomes the Risk Manager's tool and we're just passive recipients of alerts, adoption will be zero; we need to feel like active participants
openQuestions: When I log in, do I automatically see only my department's corrective actions and compliance tasks, or do I have to filter a full list?
What does closing a corrective action look like for me — how many steps, what fields, and what evidence do I need to attach?
If I delegate a corrective action to one of my team members, what do they see when they log in, and do I stay notified on its progress?
Can I see what triggered a corrective action — the original incident or risk finding — without having to ask the Risk Manager to explain it?
What's the notification format — a plain email with a direct link, an in-app alert, or something else — and can I choose which ones I get?
If I close a corrective action and the Risk Manager disagrees with the closure, what happens — does it reopen automatically, and am I notified with a reason?
How do I request a deadline extension if my team hits a legitimate obstacle, and who needs to approve it?
Can I see a list of everything my department is responsible for — risks, compliance obligations, corrective actions — in one place, sorted by due date?
If the same type of corrective action keeps coming up in my department, does the system show me that history so I can raise the underlying issue?
What does the evidence attachment process look like — can I upload a PDF, a photo, or a completed checklist directly from my browser?
Can my team members see only their own assigned corrective actions, or do they see the full department list?
If there's a compliance deadline I'm not aware of — say, an annual review that the Risk Manager set up — will I be notified in advance or only when it's overdue?
Is there a way to mark a corrective action as blocked rather than overdue when we're waiting on something outside my team's control?
How long does a typical new user take to get up to speed — what's the realistic learning curve for someone who's never used a GRC platform before?
If I have a question about a specific risk or compliance obligation in my department's register, can I look it up myself, or do I need to ask the Risk Manager?
